title: How Would You Interpret Kaworu If
display_title: How Would You Interpret Kaworu If
wikitext:
[]: [SPOILERS] How Would You Interpret Kaworu If...             Quote Post by The Killer of Heroes » Fri Mar 01, 2013 11:19 pm Okay, imagine if in Final we see ABSOLUTELY NOTHING of Kaworu. No alternate version, no clone, no reincarnation. NOTHING. Nothing at all to suggest he even could come back... How would you then interpret his final "We will meet again" message to Shinji in 3.0, EvaGeeks Forum?

[]:             Quote Post by TheFriskyIan » Fri Mar 01, 2013 11:34 pm We'll meet again Shinji-kun....IN DEATH!!!!!

[]:             Quote Post by Hiten777 » Sat Mar 02, 2013 12:17 am Something tells me that the main focus for 4.0 will be finishing what Shinji was trying to do in 3.0, undo the damage and set things right. How this will be accomplished is up for debate, but I believe when that happens Shinji will be able to go back to a specific time in the past and fix his mistake. That being said, my theory is that Shinji will go back to the point where Unit-01 was awakening. Why? Because that is when 3I started. However that also means that Shinji will once again meet Kaworu from the past. I'm not sure how much of 1.0 and 2.0 are still gonna be relevant, but in the first movie we see that Kaworu has certain memories that carry over to his other possible clones (aka the empty and closed caskets next to him). He stated, "The third again? You never change. I look forward to meeting you Shinji Ikari." I have a feeling that Kaworu in 3.0 already figured that something would happen and cause Shinji to meet him in another body or possibly after he revealed the way to use the spears to undo the mistakes of the past.

[]: Re: [SPOILERS] How Would You Interpret Kaworu If...             Quote Post by palerider1911 » Sat Mar 02, 2013 12:28 am The Killer of Heroes wrote: Okay, imagine if in Final we see ABSOLUTELY NOTHING of Kaworu. No alternate version, no clone, no reincarnation. NOTHING. Nothing at all to suggest he even could come back... How would you then interpret his final "We will meet again" message to Shinji in 3.0, EvaGeeks Forum?

[]: Re: [SPOILERS] How Would You Interpret Kaworu If...             Quote Post by CJD » Sat Mar 02, 2013 12:33 am palerider1911 wrote: I've always taken much of what Kaworu has said throughout NME as instances of "breaking the 4th wall ".

[]:             Quote Post by The Killer of Heroes » Mon Mar 04, 2013 3:40 pm Yeah, it might be the kind of trollingly ambiguous thing Anno would put in there just to have us scratching our heads. Can't say I ever thought of Kaworu's lines as a reference to the afterlife before though.

[]:             Quote Post by CJD » Mon Mar 04, 2013 5:13 pm The Killer of Heroes wrote: Can't say I ever thought of Kaworu's lines as a reference to the afterlife before though.

[]:             Quote Post by Clover » Mon Mar 04, 2013 5:30 pm Hiten777 wrote: Something tells me that the main focus for 4.0 will be finishing what Shinji was trying to do in 3.0, undo the damage and set things right. How this will be accomplished is up for debate, but I believe when that happens Shinji will be able to go back to a specific time in the past and fix his mistake.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Mon Mar 04, 2013 5:57 pm I tend to feel that the idea of a magic repair of the world is too far out, even for the Eva universe - and so Kaworu was fooled from the start (so that he will carry Shinji on into doing what Gendou wants), not by being told the wrong spears, but by being told the whole thing at all. His naivety, as a non-human, enabled him to accept this, but at the end logic is telling him that this isn't going to work (though he hangs this on "the wrong spears"). So there really is no repair to be retried in 4 (as the subtitle of 3 has told us already), and Shinji's redemption has to be achieved some other way. This may, I suppose, involve Kaworu somehow, just as EoE involved Kaworu after his death in the continuity - and this would justify him saying they would meet again.

[]:             Quote Post by Yokai » Wed Mar 06, 2013 8:08 am Kaworu is saying he'll meet Shinji again because, well, it happened once before so why wouldn't it happen again? And before you deny the sequel theory, I just mean that he states earlier in the series that this is indeed their second encounter. Since Anno believes that Evangelion is about repeating the same mistakes again and again, it seems like it would only be fitting for him to say "We'll meet again" even if they never do. It could be that Shinji puts an end to this -- or not. Shinji...

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Wed Mar 06, 2013 3:40 pm Because saying goodbye is too sad.

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Wed Mar 06, 2013 3:44 pm Kaworu's all over EOE as a metaphysical character. He'll probably show up as some kind of all knowing spirit like that other Rei we've glimpsed from time to time.

[]: Re: [SPOILERS] How Would You Interpret Kaworu If...             Quote Post by amitakartok » Wed Mar 06, 2013 6:15 pm palerider1911 wrote: Like...he seems aware about his pre-NME existance, and about the way that the plot turned out the last time around.

[]:             Quote Post by Güf Trüp » Wed Mar 06, 2013 7:38 pm AuraTwilight wrote: Because saying goodbye is too sad.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Wed Mar 06, 2013 7:43 pm Maybe we'll get some form of Quantum Kaworu.

[]:             Quote Post by Hiten777 » Thu Mar 07, 2013 8:32 pm Clover wrote: I'm usually against saying this, but I think you missed the point of 3.0. Maybe he can start fixing things, but he can't go back and redo it.

[]: Re: [SPOILERS] How Would You Interpret Kaworu If...             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Fri Mar 08, 2013 4:39 am The Killer of Heroes wrote: Okay, imagine if in Final we see ABSOLUTELY NOTHING of Kaworu. No alternate version, no clone, no reincarnation. NOTHING. Nothing at all to suggest he even could come back... How would you then interpret his final "We will meet again" message to Shinji in 3.0, EvaGeeks Forum?

[]: Re: [SPOILERS] How Would You Interpret Kaworu If...             Quote Post by Stan » Sun Mar 10, 2013 7:58 pm CJD wrote: Well, it's either that or there's some time foolery going on. I admit the former is possible but I do hope it's not the case. Anyway, if we get nothing of Kaworu I'll just take it as a ringing endorsement of the existence of an afterlife. Either way I'm not expecting Kaworu's hints to be explained in 4.0, and I think anyone who is best temper their hopes. I highly expect we'll be debating the meaning of his words 15 years from now when Rebuild of Rebuild of Evangelion 1.0 comes out in theaters.

[]:             Quote Post by one-eyed » Mon Mar 11, 2013 6:18 am Everything will depend on how much "angel" is Kaworu. If he is inhuman enough, he will have a core and if we use the other angels as an example it is located in the chest. An angel dies only when its core is destroyed; if he has a core in the chest then a head exploded may not have been enough to kill him permanently. Probably will take time to regenerate a head, but he could resurrect on the third day (in the original, Unit 01 crushed the body and decapitated Kaworu, but what killed him must have been the core crushed). However, I think unlikely Anno use this possibility, but it exists.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Mon Mar 11, 2013 7:31 am On a biological level, Kaworu is human, he doesn't have a big red core like the rest of the Angels, it's his "soul" which is angelic. That's why he can walk in NERV without setting all the alarms about a "pattern blue", unless he uses his powers. The con is that he's subject to all the weaknesses of a human body, getting crushed to death by an EVA being one! It's the same thing than with Rei : the soul of an "Angel"(Lilith) in a human body. But he could still sees Shinji in 4.0 if his soul is still wandering around, like he did in EoE, even if he was supposed to be dead then.
